home.title: Prompts
home.subtitle: Improve a prompt for the model that will actually run it.
home.emptyBody: Paste a prompt, choose the target model, and let Super-Prompt build the test set.

task.name: Name
task.descriptionPlaceholder: What the model is supposed to do, in plain words.

delete.title: Delete task?
delete.body: This will permanently delete the task, its dataset, and all runs.
delete.confirmPrompt: Type the task name to confirm:

dataset.title: Examples
dataset.subtitle: Inputs (and optional expected outputs) used to evaluate prompts.
dataset.emptyBody: Add at least 2 examples before optimizing.

generate.title: Generate dataset
generate.body: Ask the judge model to invent examples based on your task description.

extraContext.label: Extra context (optional)
extraContext.placeholder: e.g. Focus on formal business emails, 2–3 sentences each

improve.title: Improve a prompt
improve.subtitle: Paste a prompt and target model. Super-Prompt builds examples, then runs GEPA.
improve.promptPlaceholder: Paste the system prompt you use today…

detail.idle: The run starts after examples are ready.
detail.intake: Building evaluation examples and a rubric from the current prompt.
detail.preflight: Testing target, judge JSON, and mutator JSON calls.
detail.starting: Creating the run and preparing the first candidates.
detail.mutating: The mutator is creating a challenger from judge feedback.
detail.answering: The target model is answering example {current}/{total} for both prompts.
detail.judging: The judge is comparing answer pair {current}/{total}.
detail.persisting: Saving the iteration, answer pairs, and new candidate.

status.preflight: Testing target, judge, and mutator calls…
status.providerNeeded: Add an API key or use a local provider before examples can be generated.
status.manualExamplesPreserved: Manual or imported examples are preserved. Delete them first if you want generated examples.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Auto-generated from the prompt. Imported or edited examples stay untouched.
examples.empty: They will appear here after you paste a prompt.

result.title: Best prompt
result.body: Copy it, apply it as the new current prompt, or inspect the full GEPA history below.
result.emptyBody: The first run starts after examples are ready.

run.title: Optimize
run.subtitle: Watch your prompt improve, one iteration at a time.
run.summaryBody: Last completed or active run. Candidate details are collapsed unless you need to inspect them.
run.empty: Press Start to begin the optimization loop.

plan.customBody: Manual run settings are active. Restore the smart plan if you want the app to choose the defaults again.

hints.iterationsCap: How many prompt mutations to try. 1-3 is a quick smoke, 4-8 is normal for hosted cheap models, 10+ costs more but may find smaller gains.
hints.sampleSize: How many dataset examples each candidate pair is tested on. 2 is fast and noisy, 4 is a good default, 8+ is stricter and slower.
hints.concurrency: How many requests can run at once. 1 is safest for local or rate-limited models, 2-4 is usually fine for paid cloud models, 6+ can hit provider limits.
hints.earlyStop: Stops after this many iterations without a better score. 0 disables plateau stopping; 2-4 keeps runs from wasting tokens.
hints.tokenBudget: Hard token ceiling for a run. 0 means unlimited; set it when testing expensive models or a very long dataset.
hints.judgeTemp: Randomness for judging and scoring. Keep near 0-0.2 for stable comparisons; higher values make verdicts less repeatable.
hints.targetTemp: Randomness for target answers. 0-0.3 is best for summarization fidelity; higher values can add variety but more drift.
hints.mutatorTemp: Randomness for prompt mutations. 0.4-0.8 explores useful variants; 1+ can produce bigger but less predictable changes.

history.title: History
history.emptyBody: Start an optimization run to see it here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Provider, models, language.
settings.label: Label
settings.judgeModelHint: Used for scoring and prompt mutations while the separate arbitrator is off.
settings.ollamaBody: Detect a local Ollama server and use one chat model for target, judge, and mutator.
settings.demoBody: Run the full improvement process on mock models without an API key or network.
settings.localNoKey: Local and demo providers can leave the API key empty.
settings.useTargetForJudge: Use target model for judge too
settings.arbitratorBody: Route judge (and mutator) calls to a different endpoint or model. Off — the default judge model is used.
settings.arbitratorHint: When enabled, both the judge and the mutator use this endpoint. The mutator always reuses the arbitrator's model.
settings.apiKeyWarning: Stored locally in plain text — visible to anyone with access to this browser.
settings.modelRateLimitsBody: Throttle chat requests per exact model slug. Applies to target, judge, mutator, preflight, intake, and dataset generation.
settings.modelRateLimitsEmpty: No configured limits. Upstream 429 cooldowns are still handled automatically.

errors.judgeParse: Judge response could not be parsed
errors.mutatorParse: Mutator response could not be parsed
errors.noProvider: Configure a provider in Settings first.
errors.noDataset: Add at least 2 examples to the dataset.
errors.quotaExceeded: Browser storage is full. Export your data and prune old runs.
errors.corsHint: If the request fails, the provider may not allow browser CORS.
